fact_verdicts.note: Action metadata matches on id, type TreasuryWithdrawals, title, proposed epoch 639, expires after 646.
Anchor onChain.gov_action.rewards key and value "9832979000000" match the metadata amount; ₳9,832,979 stated in abstract and §10.4.
Abstract: "Total ask: ₳9,832,979 ($1,868,266 at $0.19/₳)"; "funds a 18-month transition and operations"; §7 repeats 18 months including the transition period.
Abstract states the free, community-governed public good framing and the transfer of source code, trademarks, and domains into community stewardship; §4 adds associated assets.
§2 lists 39.3% (2022), 56.5% (2023), 65.5% (2024), 71.5% (2025) from the Cardano Foundation survey.
§2: 781k unique visitors, more than 1.84 billion API requests, almost 700 rps, over 7 TB of API data.
§2: >50% of transactions in most epochs; more than 100 Icebreakers joined.
§3: "90% of all Cardano traffic served through the Blockfrost API comes from the free tier."
§4: five seats, four for open-source infrastructure development entities, one community seat; elections hoped by end of year, board appointed no later than end of Q1 2027.
§4 names all five preliminary board members exactly as listed; IOG holds a non-voting, purely advisory seat during the first year.

§6 table: Milestones 1–4 spanning Q3 2026 to Q2-Q3 2027, then "Operational milestones 5–12" in 2027 with a minimum 99% monthly uptime SLA and quarterly reports covering technical metrics and budget summary.
Transcription of the §7 table is exact, including the dollar-signed values under the "Allocation (ADA)" header and the ADA values under "Allocation (USD)".
§7 prose states 1,868,266 USD and 9,832,979 ADA; the table headers are therefore inverted relative to their contents. Directly entailed by the material.
§7: team of six — one project manager, one community manager, four developers — plus overhead.
§7: entire infrastructure stack (compute, hosting, tooling) plus DevOps personnel at approximately $20,000 per month.

§7 states unspent post-transition infrastructure budget returns to the treasury; §10.5 states all funds not disbursed by the end of the delivery period are returned, with proportional return on partial delivery or scope reduction.
§10.1: off-chain Legal Contract between Input Output and Cardano Development Holdings (CDH), administered by Intersect, covering delivery schedule and dispute resolution.
§10.2: milestones, acceptance criteria, payment amounts and expected delivery dates agreed between Input Output and Intersect; acceptance supported by a 3rd Party Assurer.
§10.3: Sundae Labs treasury management smart contract framework; the 2026 TRSC stake address is stake1784sdxt6jjennmstphgdu7l7c2scf5d02a6cve2dgn5s2kq5u3j9v, identical to the on-chain reward target.

values_hierarchy.md ranks treasury stewardship (2) and evidence quality/reproducibility (3) above public-benefit ecosystem growth (4).
treasury_spending_doctrine.md, Dossier Gate Posture: "Hard gate (current): incomplete independently verified diligence means NEEDS_MORE_INFO, never an automatic NO."

treasury_spending_doctrine.md, Counterfactual risk: for established infrastructure the engine evaluates ecosystem cost of non-funding, workflow dependency, replacement difficulty, sustained operation, and verified adoption alongside spending risk.

verification.summary: All 27 dossier facts are supported by the supplied material: 27 supported, 0 contradicted, 0 not_found, and no material discrepancies. Every figure, named entity, address, and quoted phrase was checked character-for-character against the anchor document, the on-chain action metadata and context block, and the doctrine text. Two items warranted extra scrutiny and both survived it: fact 12 faithfully reproduces the anchor's Section 7 budget table including its inverted ADA/USD column headers (dollar-denominated values sit under 'Allocation (ADA)' and ADA-denominated values under 'Allocation (USD)'), and fact 13's inversion claim is directly entailed by the adjacent prose stating 1,868,266 USD and 9,832,979 ADA. Fact 11's milestone range 5-12 matches the Section 6 deliverables table, notwithstanding a separate 'Milestones 6-18' reference in the anchor's KPI section, which the dossier does not cite. The on-chain recipient stake address in facts 19 and 20 matches the anchor's declared 2026 Treasury Reserve Smart Contract stake address exactly. No figure, name, date, or commitment appears in the dossier facts that is absent from the source material.
